title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ullepitschii

This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems. side_effects: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ullepitschii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea and vomiting due to its toxic alkaloids, particularly berberine and columbamine. Prolonged consumption of the plant may also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach cramps. In severe cases, improper use of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. ullepitschii may cause heart palpitations, seizures, and even respiratory failure. It is essential to exercise caution and consult a medical professional before using any part of the plant for medicinal purposes.
